steps.title: Use corresponding angles in similar polygons
steps.content: Similar polygons have equal corresponding angles. Use the listed vertex correspondence to match $S$ with $Z$; the side lengths affect scale, not angle measure.

steps.title: Establish vertex correspondence
steps.content: We are given that $P$ corresponds to $W$, $Q$ corresponds to $X$, and $R$ corresponds to $Y$. Since the quadrilaterals are listed as $PQRS$ and $WXYZ$, vertices must correspond in order. Therefore, $S$ must correspond to $Z$.

steps.title: Use the corresponding angles property
steps.content: In similar polygons, corresponding angles are congruent. Since $S$ corresponds to $Z$, we have $\angle Z = \angle S = 120^{\circ}$. D is correct.
